raw_input:
[5.0/5] Strong Hold, Classic Scent I love the smell of an old fashioned barbershop, and this pomade captures the scent really well. The Ace High holds my hair really well, and doesn't leave it feeling greasy or slick after applying. A little bit goes a long way too, so this tub will last quite awhile. And it washes off very quickly and cleanly too. [5.0/5] I became an immediate fan This product has everything I like. A likeable backstory about the founders, produced in small hand crafted patches, and delivers the quality results that it promises.

I wear my hair short (buzzed on the sides) with about 3-4 inches on top, which I style differently depending on the day. I usually like it combed to the side, and pushed back, but with volume to give it a nice swoop. My hair is fine, but a lot of it.

I’ve never been a fan of pomade in the past because I find it doesn’t hold it’s shape and is too shiny. However, this pomade provides a natural looking shine, while holding my hair in my preferred style. The pomade itself is very light and thin. I didn’t think it would work when applying, but within seconds it began to form my hair into my go-to style. It dries to a “tacky” feel, which allows you to readjust throughout the day, but wouldn’t feel comfortable to run your fingers through it. After applying, the product easily washed off my hands, and out of my hair at the end of the day.

The scent is good but not overwhelming. It has the typical “masculine” scent that you will find in deodorants, body wash, shaving cream, etc. for men. The scent lingers till you wash it out.

Overall, I will definitely be using up this entire jar. [3.0/5] Great scent, good hold and volume, rinses poorly After having had a 1/4-inch buzzcut for about 15 years, about 18 months ago, I started growing out my hair, which is now quite long and sits around the bottom of my ear. As a result, I've been using different types of hair products to style it in different ways and keep it under control. Given a chance to try this pomade, I decided to give it a shot.

Packaging-wise, this is really simple: a glass jar with a metal lid. It has that old-timey barbershop feel, and would look nice on your counter or shelf, if you're into displaying your grooming products. Just remember it's glass, and an accidental drop on your counter or tiled floor is going to result in a disaster. The lid is a good quality and the seal seems to keep the product fresh.

As soon as you open this jar, you'll recognize that "barbershop scent". It has that minty-eucalyptus odor that's refreshing and eye-opening. There are days when I use this, when I'm sitting at my desk I wonder "what smells so good"...and I realize it's my hair. So if you're into good scents and smelling-good for yourself or others, you'll definitely like this.

The consistency of this product is quite interesting. It looks like vanilla pudding. However, it's definitely much thicker than vanilla pudding. It's not sticky. It feels and looks like a compound that would end up very oily in your hair, but that's not the result. Even given my hair's length, I don't have to use a lot of this, and typically on damp hair I only have to use about two fingertips' worth of this product in my hair.

While your mileage may vary, when I put this in my hair, it has an immediate effect. It provides immediate styling strength. It also helps to add a bit of volume, so that as I comb it through, I can easily prop-up the parts of my hair to give it a little volume and height. It dries well and doesn't get too stiff, leaving my hair with some movement, where I'm not afraid to run my fingers through it or adjust it. I'd say it's really good for my more "casual day" styles.

My only complaint, and for me this is really important, is that it doesn't rinse well in the shower. I only wash my hair every third day to maintain its health, and while nearly every other product I use rinses almost cleanly without leaving much of a residue, this particular product won't rinse cleanly. This becomes a problem if I use this on day two, but want to use a different product on day 3, for example. It definitely adds to residual build-up. I realize not every product rinses cleanly with just water, but this one is particularly problematic, forcing me to either wash my hair earlier or plan the days on which I use this so the build-up doesn't become an issue on consecutive days. So, if like me, you don't like washing your hair often, this may be a deal-breaker.

Overall, though, I'm quite impressed. The small jar is actually going to last me quite some time. It has a great scent and styling power. I'd probably use it every day if it weren't for the rinsing issue. [5.0/5] Excellent Hold When I first opened the jar, I groaned a little. Judging by the consistency of the product, I thought it was going to be more of a styling cream, than a conventional pomade. It felt too loose and watery.

I'm not really sure how, but this stuff changes dramatically when you apply it your hair. It firms right up and makes it very easy to put your hair in place and hold it there.

When it drys, it holds well. It's not as stiff as hair gel and has a little bit of flex, but it should hold up well, unless it's very, very windy outside.

A little bit goes a long way. I don't doubt that this jar will last a few months. Then again, I don't have a lot of hair.

There is not much of a scent and once it's applied, you won't notice it at all.

I'd say this is the best pomade I've found and even beats out American Crew in terms of quality and value. [4.0/5] Works Great! Ace High, High Noon Pomade works well on both my beard and hair. It left my hair feeling flat, which is a bit of a bummer. However, it has a pleasant and mild scent. A little bit goes a long way with this product. Also, it's worth pointing out that this product is made in the USA and comes out of Detroit, MI. Overall, this worked great! [5.0/5] Great hold Product works great and holds my hair in place well while I’m working [4.0/5] Nice hold First impression is that the smell is mainly eucalyptus based, but not too strong. The texture out of the can is at first very creamy, which I thought may affect the holding power, but once I worked in a couple of fingertip sized scoops into my hair, I found that the pomade started to take hold and firm up. My hair held its shape throughout the day and was easy to fix with a comb if needed. When washing it out, despite containing oils and wax, regular shampoo seemed to do the trick. Little to no residue leftover in my hair after a shower. [5.0/5] Great The best pomade out there. Period. [5.0/5] Excellent Love this stuff. Once I figured out how much to use, since it is more like a cream than a wax, it’s been fantastic. Smells like that classic barber shop smell. I’ll certainly buy this again. [5.0/5] Love the scent Love the scent! Wish I could get it in a cologne! Stuff works great been using it for a few years now and don’t use any other brand. [5.0/5] Best I’ve found so far! High quality product that actually holds all day. Lasted me a year. It is not super shiny, but not matte either. It is a reasonable balance. I’d recommend trying it! [5.0/5] Does a great job on short hair Great product. Non greasy. Easy to blend into hair. Nice scent. [4.0/5] Thicker, oil based; smells great! This was a big change from my previous pomade which was water based and lighter. Ace High is much thicker and oil based. I like it but would prefer if it were a little lighter. This stuff just grabs on to my hair as soon as my fingers make contact. It holds really well and I got two compliments the first day I put it in. I found it takes a few washes to come out. Unfortunately my wife doesn't like it and says it looks too greasy. So I'll use it on work days but avoid it going out with my wife.

So if you want a more natural, thicker and slicker pomade, I recommend this! And my son, daughter and I all agree it smells great too. My teen daughter liked it so much she said when she starts dating she wants him to wear this stuff! [4.0/5] Gentle hold, nice shine. LOVE the natural ingredients and scent of this product for the men and boy folk in my house. However, we have found that it performs best in cool dry weather. It doesn't hold so good (and we haven't found a product that does so I still say this is still a great product). We got two from this brand, the clay and the pomade. We use the clay for lift on the thick hair and the pomade for a smoother look on the thinner hair. Both are well liked in our house.

Thanks for reading. I hope this review is helpful! [3.0/5] Great product but could smell a lot better It’s great for my hair but it smells like pine sol… [4.0/5] Good, But I Disagree On The Scent This is among the best holding pomades I've used and it washes out easily. I appreciate the glass jar, the hand crafted presentation, and the feeling that it uses natural ingredients and I'm not slathering a ton of chemicals on my dome. But — and this is certainly subjective — I find the scent to be a lot more "Grandma's Medicine Cabinet" than "Grandpa's Barbershop". And it's not exactly a subtle scent, either. I mean you kind of have to love it, cause it's going to hit you square in the mirror each morning. I'm glad they make an unscented version; going with that next time. [5.0/5] Wax Feel, Easy to Wash Off You don't need much to get a strong hold in your hair. It really feels like a thick wax based pomade, but you can easily wash it off your hair with one round of shampoo. Fragrance is a bit like cologne. If you're looking for a clean cut look with a modest shine in your hair, this is a good buy. [3.0/5] Pine Sol Scent with decent hold The hold is decent. Scoopability is a breeze. Scoops like cake batter. But the scent...... exactly like Pine Sol cleaning solution!!!! Guys, please change this scent!!!! You’ll sell more if you do!

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews for Ace High High Noon Pomade:

Overall, the product is very well received, with most reviewers giving it 4 or 5 stars. The hold is consistently praised, with many noting that it firms up nicely once applied to hair, despite having a loose, creamy consistency straight from the jar. Nearly everyone agrees that a little goes a long way, with some saying a single jar can last months or even up to a year.

The scent is a divisive topic. Many reviewers love the classic barbershop or eucalyptus-mint smell, with some wishing it came in a cologne. However, a handful of reviewers compared it unfavorably to Pine Sol or a medicine cabinet, and at least one said they plan to switch to the unscented version.

The texture surprised several reviewers who expected it to perform poorly based on its creamy, pudding-like appearance, but found it held their hair well throughout the day. It provides a natural-looking shine that is neither too glossy nor too matte, and leaves some flexibility rather than a stiff, crunchy finish.

Washability is a mixed point. Some found it rinsed out easily with one round of shampoo, while others noted it required multiple washes and could leave residue or buildup, which was a dealbreaker for those who do not wash their hair daily.

The glass jar packaging and handcrafted, small-batch production were appreciated by several buyers who liked the natural ingredients and overall presentation.
